# history.example.txt — example of what history.txt looks like after several uses
#
# This file is auto-generated by Voxtral Paste when ENABLE_HISTORY=true.
# It is populated after each MEDIUM (90–240 words) or LONG (> 240 words) transcription.
# Each bullet carries a [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] timestamp added automatically by the application.
# The model reads the existing history and merges it with new facts on each update:
#   - old facts with their timestamps are preserved as-is
#   - new facts receive today's date
#   - duplicates and outdated facts are removed by the model
#
# Entries are INDEPENDENT — the user may work on several unrelated projects
# in parallel. Facts from different entries are not necessarily related.
#
# You can consult and manually edit this file at any time.
# Copy this file to history.txt to pre-populate your context before first use.
# history.txt is listed in .gitignore — it stays on your machine and is never committed.

- [2026-03-01 09:14:22] Developer working on Voxtral Paste, a voice-to-text tool using the Mistral API
- [2026-03-01 09:14:22] Stack for Voxtral Paste: Python, Bash, Mistral API
- [2026-03-04 11:03:47] Uses pytest for testing; test suite currently has 69 tests passing
- [2026-03-06 15:28:05] Also working on a separate project: REST API with FastAPI and PostgreSQL
- [2026-03-06 15:28:05] FastAPI project uses Pydantic Settings for configuration management
- [2026-03-08 10:41:33] Working on the history context enrichment feature for Voxtral Paste
- [2026-03-08 10:41:33] Routing thresholds: SHORT < 90 words, LONG ≥ 240 words
